title: IDF Kills Hamas Anti-Tank Commander Kamal Ayash in Nuseirat Strike
detail: Israeli forces struck and killed Kamal Ayash, a key Hamas anti-tank unit commander, in a targeted strike on Nuseirat in central Gaza on March 24, 2026. The strike also killed four civilians including two boys and a pregnant woman, according to Palestinian health officials. The IDF confirmed the strike as part of ongoing operations against Hamas military infrastructure despite the ceasefire framework. The killing continues Israel's systematic targeting of Hamas's reconstituted military leadership — following the deaths of Yahya Sinwar (October 16, 2024), Ismail Haniyeh (July 31, 2024), and Mohammed Deif (confirmed January 2025). Hamas's military wing remains partially functional despite sustained attrition.

title: UN Security Council Briefed on Gaza Situation and US Recovery Plan
detail: The UN Security Council held a hearing on March 24, 2026 focused on the Gaza situation and the US-backed recovery plan. Secretary-General António Guterres and humanitarian coordinators briefed council members on conditions in Gaza. The UN Gaza reconstruction cost estimate of $50+ billion was presented, with only minimal international funding pledged. The Board of Peace — a US-led reconstruction governance framework with 27 signatory countries — was discussed as the primary vehicle for Gaza recovery. Council members debated the status of Phase Two ceasefire implementation and the absence of a concrete Palestinian governance plan for post-Hamas Gaza.
